title: rootless networking error on container startup since upgrade to F42
body:
### Issue Description

At GNOME we run a set of gitlab-runners runners that support the Project's CI/CD needs, since the upgrade to Fedora 42 (this was working fine with Fedora 40) there seems to be a race condition that causes:

ERROR: Preparation failed: creating cache volume: set volume permissions: running permission container "c7309ea6d35f49a8d68ba660c62b676929b5744844bffd75151c664ea8fe1a28" for volume "runner-qy9jf-zq7-project-9004-concurrent-1-8c5c3832aa81ed63-cache-c33bcaa1fd2c77edfc3893b41966cea8": starting permission container: Error response from daemon: rootless netns: mount resolv.conf to "/run/user/991/containers/networks/rootless-netns/run/systemd/resolve/stub-resolv.conf": no such file or directory (linux_set.go:105:0s)

On top of this any container started from the podman user systemd session doesn't seem to create a persistent /run/user/$uid/containers/networks/rootless-netns directory structure, every container that is started as a build creates a brand new rootless-netns directory and associated files (i.e resolv.conf) which then gets deleted once the build has finished. This however sometimes leave the rootless-netns directory in an inconsistent state which then prevents the subsequent build to succeed due to the error above. Generally manually running "rm -rf /run/user/$uid/containers/networks/rootless-netns" temporarily fixes the issue.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. Set up your system via https://github.com/gnome-infra/ansible/blob/master/roles/gitlab-runner/tasks/main.yml
2. Trigger new container creation via GitLab CI/CD
3. At some point the container creation process will fail with a similar error as "ERROR: Preparation failed: creating cache volume: set volume permissions: running permission container "c7309ea6d35f49a8d68ba660c62b676929b5744844bffd75151c664ea8fe1a28" for volume "runner-qy9jf-zq7-project-9004-concurrent-1-8c5c3832aa81ed63-cache-c33bcaa1fd2c77edfc3893b41966cea8": starting permission container: Error response from daemon: rootless netns: mount resolv.conf to "/run/user/991/containers/networks/rootless-netns/run/systemd/resolve/stub-resolv.conf": no such file or directory (linux_set.go:105:0s)"


### Describe the results you received

Starting a new build fails with "ERROR: Preparation failed: creating cache volume: set volume permissions: running permission container "c7309ea6d35f49a8d68ba660c62b676929b5744844bffd75151c664ea8fe1a28" for volume "runner-qy9jf-zq7-project-9004-concurrent-1-8c5c3832aa81ed63-cache-c33bcaa1fd2c77edfc3893b41966cea8": starting permission container: Error response from daemon: rootless netns: mount resolv.conf to "/run/user/991/containers/networks/rootless-netns/run/systemd/resolve/stub-resolv.conf": no such file or directory (linux_set.go:105:0s)"

### Describe the results you expected

A successful container creation

### podman info output

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

Rootless

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

On premise bare metal podman installation, containers are initialized via the gitlab-runner service:

[podman@hetzner-1 ~]$ cat .config/systemd/user/default.target.wants/gitlab-runner.service 
[Unit]
Description=GitLab Runner
Documentation=man:podman-generate-systemd(1)
Wants=network-online.target
After=network-online.target
RequiresMountsFor=/run/user/991/containers

[Service]
Environment=PODMAN_SYSTEMD_UNIT=%n
Restart=always
TimeoutStopSec=70
ExecStartPre=-rm -f /run/user/991/%n.ctr-id
ExecStart=/usr/bin/podman \
	--url unix:/run/user/991/podman/podman.sock container run \
	--cidfile=/run/user/991/%n.ctr-id \
	--cgroups=no-conmon \
	--rm \
	--attach STDOUT \
	--attach STDERR \
	--sdnotify=conmon \
	--replace \
	--name gitlab-runner \
	--volume /run/user/991/podman/podman.sock:/var/run/docker.sock:z \
	--volume /home/podman/gitlab-runner:/etc/gitlab-runner:Z \
	--label io.containers.autoupdate=image \
	--label org.gnome.pydocuum.skip=True registry.gitlab.com/gitlab-org/gitlab-runner:latest
ExecStop=/usr/bin/podman stop \
	--ignore -t 10 \
	--cidfile=/run/user/991/%n.ctr-id
ExecStopPost=/usr/bin/podman rm \
	-f \
	--ignore -t 10 \
	--cidfile=/run/user/991/%n.ctr-id
NotifyAccess=all

[Install]
WantedBy=default.target

### Additional information

Additional information like issue happens only occasionally or issue happens with a particular architecture or on a particular setting

comments.body: @Luap99 PTAL

> Set up your system via https://github.com/gnome-infra/ansible/blob/master/roles/gitlab-runner/tasks/main.yml
> Trigger new container creation via GitLab CI/CD

Sorry but this is not something we consider a reproducer, I have no time to do that or even the knowledge to debug whatever the CI pipelines would do. Can you create a reproducer with actual podman command or API queries that will trigger this?

A reviewer has determined we need more information to understand the reported issue.  A comment on what is missing should be provided. Be certain you:

* provide an exact reproducer where possible
* verify you have provided all relevant information - minimum is `podman info`
* answer any follow up questions

If no response to the `needs-info` is provided in 30 days, this issue may be closed by our stale bot.

For more information on reporting issues on this repository, consult our [issue guide](ISSUE.md).

@Luap99 if you have a system with podman running rootless with linger enabled and install the following systemd unit via /home/podman/.config/systemd/user/default.target.wants/gitlab-runner.service with gitlab-runner.service having the following content:

```
[Unit]
Description=GitLab Runner
Documentation=man:podman-generate-systemd(1)
Wants=network-online.target
After=network-online.target
RequiresMountsFor=/run/user/991/containers

[Service]
Environment=PODMAN_SYSTEMD_UNIT=%n
Restart=always
TimeoutStopSec=70
ExecStartPre=-rm -f /run/user/991/%n.ctr-id
ExecStart=/usr/bin/podman \
	--url unix:/run/user/991/podman/podman.sock container run \
	--cidfile=/run/user/991/%n.ctr-id \
	--cgroups=no-conmon \
	--rm \
	--attach STDOUT \
	--attach STDERR \
	--sdnotify=conmon \
	--replace \
	--name gitlab-runner \
	--volume /run/user/991/podman/podman.sock:/var/run/docker.sock:z \
	--volume /home/podman/gitlab-runner:/etc/gitlab-runner:Z \
	--label io.containers.autoupdate=image \
	--label org.gnome.pydocuum.skip=True registry.gitlab.com/gitlab-org/gitlab-runner:latest
ExecStop=/usr/bin/podman stop \
	--ignore -t 10 \
	--cidfile=/run/user/991/%n.ctr-id
ExecStopPost=/usr/bin/podman rm \
	-f \
	--ignore -t 10 \
	--cidfile=/run/user/991/%n.ctr-id
NotifyAccess=all

[Install]
WantedBy=default.target
```

From there:

```
systemctl --user start gitlab-runner
```

Then:

```
ls -al /run/user/991/containers/networks/rootless-netns/
total 0
drwx------. 2 podman podman  40 May 12 15:50 .
drwx------. 4 podman podman 120 May 12 15:50 ..
```

Any subsequent build will fail with:

```
ERROR: Preparation failed: creating cache volume: set volume permissions: running permission container "c7309ea6d35f49a8d68ba660c62b676929b5744844bffd75151c664ea8fe1a28" for volume "runner-qy9jf-zq7-project-9004-concurrent-1-8c5c3832aa81ed63-cache-c33bcaa1fd2c77edfc3893b41966cea8": starting permission container: Error response from daemon: rootless netns: mount resolv.conf to "/run/user/991/containers/networks/rootless-netns/run/systemd/resolve/stub-resolv.conf": no such file or directory (linux_set.go:105:0s)
```

If I run:

```
podman run --rm -it --entrypoint bash --network=bridge registry.access.redhat.com/ubi9/ubi:9.5-1731517889
```

This works as expected and I can see /run/user/991/containers/networks/rootless-netns being populated properly, I don't know off hand what API queries gitlab-runner uses behind the scenes, all we do is primarily mounting the podman socket to the gitlab-runner container via `run/user/991/podman/podman.sock:/var/run/docker.sock:z`. Do you have any idea on why something along the lines of a plain `podman run` recreates the rootless-netns directory and associated files correctly while whatever process gitlab-runner uses to trigger the container creation would not? This was working fine with Fedora 40.

> Do you have any idea on why something along the lines of a plain podman run recreates the rootless-netns directory and associated files correctly while whatever process gitlab-runner uses to trigger the container creation would not? This was working fine with Fedora 40.

Well the entire code has been most rewritten in the meantime so it is possible that something got broken by it.

Now an empty rootless-netns should totally be fine and as you mention a plain podman run command seem to work. CLI or API wise there should be no difference whatsoever in how the network code behaves.


https://github.com/containers/common/blob/54b90177af76122d69a6a08b6db1b305e39d4f3c/libnetwork/internal/rootlessnetns/netns_linux.go#L394-L502
The code will actually always call mkdir/open beforehand to create the resolv.conf parent dirs and file before calling mount so I really don't see how we would end up in a state where the file does not exit.  By the time we mount resolv.conf we have mounted and created other directories there as well.

> The code will actually always call mkdir/open beforehand to create the resolv.conf parent dirs and file before calling mount so I really don't see how we would end up in a state where the file does not exit. By the time we mount resolv.conf we have mounted and created other directories there as well.

Or wait I guess it doesn't complain about the destination missing but rather the source missing. 
That would make more sense since we init resolv.conf once when we start pasta and write the file there, if you remove that it will be missing. However if the dir is empty the netns file is also missing so we know we must start pasta and populate resolv.conf the first time so it still doesn't really make sense to me.

The fact that the API is behaving differently is concerning but code wise it really shouldn't. Is it possible that the service is running in a different namespace?

No, we had that issue in the past where a specific systemd service file would start both as user root and user podman causing it to break but now we explicitly make sure that doesn't happen, what I'm seeing:

```
podman    682671  0.0  0.0 206440 17112 ?        Ss   16:41   0:01 /usr/bin/pasta --config-net --dns-forward 169.254.1.1 -t none -u none -T none -U none --no-map-gw --quiet --netns /run/user/991/netns/netns-43d8c7fd-e885-cdf7-4f63-23b51c0b6e41 --map-guest-addr 169.254.1.2
podman    683156  0.0  0.0 206440 17132 ?        Ss   16:41   0:00 /usr/bin/pasta --config-net --dns-forward 169.254.1.1 -t none -u none -T none -U none --no-map-gw --quiet --netns /run/user/991/netns/netns-e091af74-19c0-08b6-5b11-d1c15c086918 --map-guest-addr 169.254.1.2
podman    829234  0.0  0.0 206440 17012 ?        Ss   17:28   0:00 /usr/bin/pasta --config-net --pid /run/user/991/containers/networks/rootless-netns/rootless-netns-conn.pid --dns-forward 169.254.1.1 -t none -u none -T none -U none --no-map-gw --quiet --netns /run/user/991/containers/networks/rootless-netns/rootless-netns --map-guest-addr 169.254.1.2
```

```
[root@hetzner-1 ~]# ls -al /run/user/991/containers/networks/rootless-netns/
total 4
drwx------. 3 podman podman 100 May 12 17:37 .
drwx------. 4 podman podman 120 May 12 17:28 ..
-rw-------. 1 podman podman   0 May 12 17:28 rootless-netns
-rw-------. 1 podman podman   7 May 12 17:28 rootless-netns-conn.pid
drwx------. 4 podman podman  80 May 12 17:37 run
```

From here:

```
kill 829234 # pasta pid
```

Then:

```
podman run --rm -it --entrypoint bash --network=bridge registry.access.redhat.com/ubi9/ubi:9.5-1731517889
WARN[0000] rootless netns program no longer running, trying to start it again 

[root@hetzner-1 ~]# ls -al /run/user/991/containers/networks/rootless-netns/
total 16
drwx------. 3 podman podman 160 May 12 17:52 .
drwx------. 4 podman podman 120 May 12 17:52 ..
-rw-r--r--. 1 podman podman 121 May 12 17:52 info.json
-rw-------. 1 podman podman   1 May 12 17:53 ref-count
-rw-r--r--. 1 podman podman  85 May 12 17:52 resolv.conf
-rw-------. 1 podman podman   0 May 12 17:52 rootless-netns
-rw-------. 1 podman podman   7 May 12 17:52 rootless-netns-conn.pid
drwx------. 4 podman podman  80 May 12 17:52 run
```

Then:

```
[root@3d58977c7918 /]# 
exit

ls -al /run/user/991/containers/networks/rootless-netns/
no such file or directory

systemctl --user restart gitlab-runner # can really be any systemd unit file that starts a rootless container

ls -al /run/user/991/containers/networks/rootless-netns/
empty
```

At this point builds start to break until `rmdir /run/user/991/containers/networks/rootless-netns/`.  Also noticed:

```
May 12 15:16:29 hetzner-1 systemd-coredump[632458]: Process 387634 (podman) of user 991 terminated abnormally with signal 11/SEGV, processing...
May 12 15:16:29 hetzner-1 systemd-coredump[632459]: Process 387634 (podman) of user 991 dumped core.
                                                    Module /usr/bin/podman from rpm podman-5.4.2-1.fc42.x86_64
                                                    #0  0x000055c66678f538 runtime.mallocgcSmallNoscan (/usr/bin/podman + 0x1b538)
                                                    #1  0x000055c6667eda59 runtime.mallocgc (/usr/bin/podman + 0x79a59)
                                                    #2  0x000055c6667f31a9 runtime.growslice (/usr/bin/podman + 0x7f1a9)
                                                    #3  0x000055c6667ea176 runtime.vgetrandomPutState (/usr/bin/podman + 0x76176)
                                                    #4  0x000055c6667be145 runtime.mexit (/usr/bin/podman + 0x4a145)
                                                    #5  0x000055c6667bddff runtime.mstart0 (/usr/bin/podman + 0x49dff)
```

Which may potentially explain why we're also seeing the podman systemd unit restarting every few hours:

```
May 12 15:16:29 hetzner-1 systemd-coredump[632458]: Process 387634 (podman) of user 991 terminated abnormally with signal 11/SEGV, processing...
May 12 15:16:29 hetzner-1 systemd-coredump[632459]: Process 387634 (podman) of user 991 dumped core.
May 12 15:21:03 hetzner-1 systemd-coredump[636457]: Process 632471 (podman) of user 991 terminated abnormally with signal 11/SEGV, processing...
May 12 15:21:03 hetzner-1 systemd-coredump[636458]: Process 632471 (podman) of user 991 dumped core.
May 12 17:28:10 hetzner-1 systemd-coredump[829235]: Process 682634 (podman) of user 991 terminated abnormally with signal 11/SEGV, processing...
May 12 17:28:11 hetzner-1 systemd-coredump[829236]: Process 682634 (podman) of user 991 dumped core.
```

Happy to provide any additional information you may need, thanks for your help!

Ack yeah crashing could likely lead to a weird state, stack trace looks like https://github.com/golang/go/issues/73141#issuecomment-2847963929

What is the exact podman rpm version? Your info shows `GoVersion: go1.24.1` which likely means it is still the buggy version

```
[podman@hetzner-1 ~]$ rpm -qa | grep podman
podman-5.4.2-1.fc42.x86_64
podman-docker-5.4.2-1.fc42.noarch
```

yeah I think we need to rebuild podman with a newer go, but given we release v5.5.0 final tomorrow that likely doesn't make sense and we just build that one instead with the new golang then.

If you like to confirm you can install 5.5.0-rc2 right now https://bodhi.fedoraproject.org/updates/FEDORA-2025-fa52f863f1

---

And regardless of the go bug I still should understand the full failure sequence here so we can make the rootless-netns recover from any point as the process could be killed for plenty of reasons and we certainly don't want all future commands to run into this error.

I've installed the rc2 on a couple of runners, will report back in a few hours/one day to see how it goes, available if you need any additional information if you want to dig the inconsistent behavior further.

I don't have time to debug this fully, but I guess once I do I can just insert os.Exit() calls in the code paths there to find the ones where we are unable to recover from an early exit.
But overall it would still be nice to know why you can observe different behaviors between API and CLI.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.

This is still a problem with podman 5.7.1 on fedora42

Using strace and then searching for EACCES or netns: 
"level=error msg="\"setting up Pasta: pasta failed with exit code 1:\\nnetns dir open"
without further info about what happened

The workaround is also still valid:  
`rm -rf /run/user/$UID/containers/networks/rootless-netns`

Please investigate further
@slartibart70 Without you providing a reproducer (and all required bug information, i.e. podman info) we can do nothing about it

Yes, of course (i just wanted to 'revive' this issue).

There is not that much info i can provide: Starting two quadlet-containers (frontend, backend) gives this sparse journal-log:
```
Jan 07 10:20:08 AZ-CBC-VM-BetaSwat auto-dossier-backend[39250]: Copying config sha256:8fc7e194da838bd453a8703420c7219ed4e4610de587d026d09553309cfe1a1f
Jan 07 10:20:08 AZ-CBC-VM-BetaSwat auto-dossier-backend[39250]: Writing manifest to image destination
Jan 07 10:20:08 AZ-CBC-VM-BetaSwat podman[39250]: 2026-01-07 10:20:08.60396099 +0100 CET m=+5.213806433 image pull 8fc7e194da838bd453a8703420c7219ed4e4610de587d026d09553309cfe1a1f harbor.sws.internal:>
Jan 07 10:20:08 AZ-CBC-VM-BetaSwat auto-dossier-ui[39251]: Copying config sha256:c98db9f81a18ca84ef5aebf00dd2df1874e950f524c5e906c25861493d1c6ba6
Jan 07 10:20:08 AZ-CBC-VM-BetaSwat podman[39250]: 2026-01-07 10:20:08.766270631 +0100 CET m=+5.376116015 container create e9153812f51becdf97ecf0a4f2a9b551edc1cba12c0920e2ec148653f0f4be62 (image=harbor>
Jan 07 10:20:08 AZ-CBC-VM-BetaSwat pasta[39547]: netns dir open: Permission denied, exiting
Jan 07 10:20:08 AZ-CBC-VM-BetaSwat auto-dossier-ui[39251]: Writing manifest to image destination
Jan 07 10:20:08 AZ-CBC-VM-BetaSwat podman[39250]: 2026-01-07 10:20:08.842251862 +0100 CET m=+5.452097245 container remove e9153812f51becdf97ecf0a4f2a9b551edc1cba12c0920e2ec148653f0f4be62 (image=harbor>
Jan 07 10:20:08 AZ-CBC-VM-BetaSwat auto-dossier-backend[39250]: Error: setting up Pasta: pasta failed with exit code 1:
Jan 07 10:20:08 AZ-CBC-VM-BetaSwat auto-dossier-backend[39250]: netns dir open: Permission denied, exiting
Jan 07 10:20:08 AZ-CBC-VM-BetaSwat systemd[1091]: auto-dossier-backend.service: Main process exited, code=exited, status=126/n/a
```

On another system (other quadlets) i initially could not even pinpoint the problem stemming from the log, i used strace to get the real cause: please see above, the only takeaway of strace was the location this was happening at: the `/rootless-netns` folder

As this is only on a tempfs, my best guess was to delete the folder in the hope that podman would recreate it (as it did)

I did not see any se-linux related errors when checking

```
$ podman info
host:
  arch: amd64
  buildahVersion: 1.42.2
  cgroupControllers:
  - cpu
  - memory
  - pids
  cgroupManager: systemd
  cgroupVersion: v2
  conmon:
    package: conmon-2.1.13-1.fc42.x86_64
    path: /usr/bin/conmon
```

Podman/quadlets running rootless, nothing else is running in this useraccount

```
cat /etc/os-release 
NAME="Fedora Linux"
VERSION="42 (Cloud Edition)"
RELEASE_TYPE=stable
ID=fedora
VERSION_ID=42
VERSION_CODENAME=""
PLATFORM_ID="platform:f42"
PRETTY_NAME="Fedora Linux 42 (Cloud Edition)"
```

Reproducing this error is also difficult:
Once you delete the /rootless-netns folder, any stop/restart of the quadlets happen correctly, no errors.

One thing that was similar on both systems: i did an os-upgrade, which also upgraded podman 5.6.4 to 5.7.1.
Before the upgrade, all user@id.service were stopped, then 'dnf up --refresh'. Then reboot.

The first run of a (previously running quadlet) then gives the netns errors. 
What i cannot really understand is, this is a tempfs (/run/user/...). After reboot, this should be empty and the folder-structures needed should be freshly created by podman?
How come that i need to delete /rootless-netns by hand first before it's working again?

Another thing: those are lingering useraccounts, the target in charge for auto-running this stack is
```
cat auto-dossier.target 
[Unit]
Description=Auto-Dossier application stack
Wants=auto-dossier-network.service
After=auto-dossier-network.service
Wants=auto-dossier-ui.service auto-dossier-backend.service

[Install]
WantedBy=default.target
```

Maybe this is the culprit? Not really logging in, but service-starts because of lingering useraccounts and install:default.target?

> netns dir open: Permission denied, exiting

That is almost certainly selinux in my experience, we had quite some trouble with these weird transition rules from podman over to pasta. Maybe the denials are dontaudit. To properly test I disable selinux to be sure normally in such cases.

My guess is the first startup has the wrong selinux labels on some of the files thus it fails, when you delete it again it then gets recreated with the right labels.
Before deleting the dir it would be helpful if you could check the label it has, `ls -Z $XDG_RUNTIME_DIR/containers/networks/`?

I do have a very similar setup on one of my systems (f42, lingering and systemd starting the services) and have not noticed any pasta startup issues there.

i'm running out of upgradeable systems right now :-D

What i did to check for selinux problems was `ausearch -m AVC,USER_AVC` - but without any usable results.
I also did check the fs-lables (but, did not write them down, sorry)

right now, it looks like this:

> My guess is the first startup has the wrong selinux labels on some of the files thus it fails, when you delete it again it then gets recreated with the right labels.

i did reboot the system, the quadlets come up as expected.
Another Heisenbug?

As described above: once the folder is deleted, and recreated, all is fine. Either starting the target manually (in the useraccount) or by rebooting.
It **seems** it's happening only after upgrade (until i have other proof, that is...)
